evidence_packet.evidence_text: Economic Development Andy Burnham wins Makerfield: When will the next Greater Manchester mayor be elected now he is an MP? Huge win in by-election paves way for Labour leadership challenge Economic Development Declan Carey Local Democracy Reporter 07:33, 19 Jun 2026 Andy Burnham Andy Burnham is heading back to Parliament after winning the Makerfield by-election The newly elected Labour MP came out on top in the race against a strong challenge from Reform UK candidate Robert Kenyon But overnight Mr Burnham secured a seismic win, with 24,927 votes placing him well ahead of Reform challenger Robert Kenyon who secured 15,696 votes. His return to Parliament is expected to pave the way for a leadership challenge against Prime Minister Keir Starmer It marks the end of weeks of campaigning in the constituency based south of Wigan, and the start of the next political race in Greater Manchester for Andy Burnham's old job as mayor Becoming an MP disqualifies Burnham from being Greater Manchester mayor, so a replacement needs to be found for the region's top political job

evidence_texts: Political parties have begun to announce their candidates for the forthcoming Greater Manchester mayoral by-election The by-election was triggered when Andy Burnham resigned as mayor following his return to the House of Commons after winning the Makerfield by-election on 18 June Labour's Burnham won the first mayoral election in 2017 and secured two further terms in office People living in all 10 boroughs of Greater Manchester will elect his successor on 30 July Here is a list (in alphabetical order, by surname) of all of the candidates who have confirmed they are standing in the election This list will be updated as and when further candidates are announced

Andy Burnham's victory in the Makerfield by-election means he has to step down as Greater Manchester mayor Burnham has been mayor since the role was first created in 2017 and the race to become his successor will be fiercely contested by all involved But what is the role they will be fighting for? The role of mayor was created as part of a law passed in 2016, one of a number of big pieces of devolution legislation brought in by the Conservatives after taking power in 2010 In a 2012 referendum, Manchester rejected having a directly-elected city mayor, along with eight other major cities, but the decision to have a regional or metropolitan mayor was directly negotiated between the government and the 10 Greater Manchester councils Andy Burnham, who left Parliament to stand for the role, was chosen as Labour's candidate for the first mayoral election in 2017 and won, taking 63.4% of the vote

Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade. Credit: PA Images Andy Burnham’s historic victory in the Makerfield by-election sets off a rapid chain reaction for both Greater Manchester and the wider UK political landscape A metro mayor cannot simultaneously serve as an MP, a clear legal and electoral process is now underway to find someone new to take on the role Andy Burnham has won a seat as an MP - but can he still be the Mayor of Greater Manchester? No. Andy Burnham cannot be mayor and a member of parliament. He had seven days - from the moment of his election - to resign from the post which he has held for almost a decade He handed in his resignation on 19 June - triggering the process of the by-election Burnham cannot do both jobs because the Mayor of Greater Manchester holds Police and Crime Commissioner (PCC) powers and UK l

similar_previous.semantic_text: Andy Burnham sworn in as new Makerfield MP The former Greater Manchester mayor is seen as favourite to succeed Sir Keir Starmer as prime minister The former Greater Manchester mayor is seen as favourite to succeed Sir Keir Starmer as prime minister Учитывать, что сегодня это влияет на повестку городской политики. Just hours earlier, Sir Keir Starmer announced his resignation both as prime minister and Labour leader Former Greater Manchester Mayor Burnham, who won the Makerfield by-election by 9,000 votes last week after seeing off a challenge from Reform UK, has confirmed he wants to succeed Starmer The by-election was triggered when Labour's Josh Simons stood down to make way for Burnham, who represented the neighbouring Greater Manchester constituency of Leigh between 2001 until 2017, when he became mayor Burnham bowed his head several times as he made his way through the Commons chamber and grinned as he was greeted by his colleagues Sir Desmond Swayne, a Conservative former minister, heckled as Burnham approached the clerks to be sworn in In another heckle, MPs were told that "he's not the messiah" and there was a shout of "water into wine"

evidence_packet.evidence_text: Just hours earlier, Sir Keir Starmer announced his resignation both as prime minister and Labour leader Former Greater Manchester Mayor Burnham, who won the Makerfield by-election by 9,000 votes last week after seeing off a challenge from Reform UK, has confirmed he wants to succeed Starmer The by-election was triggered when Labour's Josh Simons stood down to make way for Burnham, who represented the neighbouring Greater Manchester constituency of Leigh between 2001 until 2017, when he became mayor Burnham bowed his head several times as he made his way through the Commons chamber and grinned as he was greeted by his colleagues Sir Desmond Swayne, a Conservative former minister, heckled as Burnham approached the clerks to be sworn in In another heckle, MPs were told that "he's not the messiah" and there was a shout of "water into wine"

evidence_texts: Just hours earlier, Sir Keir Starmer announced his resignation both as prime minister and Labour leader Former Greater Manchester Mayor Burnham, who won the Makerfield by-election by 9,000 votes last week after seeing off a challenge from Reform UK, has confirmed he wants to succeed Starmer The by-election was triggered when Labour's Josh Simons stood down to make way for Burnham, who represented the neighbouring Greater Manchester constituency of Leigh between 2001 until 2017, when he became mayor Burnham bowed his head several times as he made his way through the Commons chamber and grinned as he was greeted by his colleagues Sir Desmond Swayne, a Conservative former minister, heckled as Burnham approached the clerks to be sworn in In another heckle, MPs were told that "he's not the messiah" and there was a shout of "water into wine"

Andy Burnham has been officially sworn in as Makerfield's new MP amid loud cheers from his Labour colleagues in the House of Commons Just hours earlier, Sir Keir Starmer announced his resignation both as prime minister and Labour leader Former Greater Manchester Mayor Burnham, who won the Makerfield by-election by 9,000 votes last week after seeing off a challenge from Reform UK, has confirmed he wants to succeed Starmer The by-election was triggered when Labour's Josh Simons stood down to make way for Burnham, who represented the neighbouring Greater Manchester constituency of Leigh between 2001 until 2017, when he became mayor Burnham bowed his head several times as he made his way through the Commons chamber and grinned as he was greeted by his colleagues Sir Desmond Swayne, a Conservative former minister, heckled as Burnham approached the clerks to be sworn in

Political parties have begun to announce their candidates for the forthcoming Greater Manchester mayoral by-election The by-election was triggered when Andy Burnham resigned as mayor following his return to the House of Commons after winning the Makerfield by-election on 18 June Labour's Burnham won the first mayoral election in 2017 and secured two further terms in office People living in all 10 boroughs of Greater Manchester will elect his successor on 30 July Here is a list (in alphabetical order, by surname) of all of the candidates who have confirmed they are standing in the election This list will be updated as and when further candidates are announced

As Andy Burnham gave his acceptance speech on the stage after he swept to victory in the Makerfield by-election, he spoke of "some sadness" that the appointment brought to an end "my wonderful nine years as mayor of Greater Manchester" He said it was a "wrench" to have to leave the job he loved, although promised he was not "leaving the service of Greater Manchester" But what is his legacy according to people living in the region? Some residents in Salford told of their sadness and disappointment at his departure, after Burnham's return to Westminster politics automatically triggered a mayoral election Neil Townley, 59, from Worsley, said: "I'm quietly disappointed to be perfectly honest with you "I've always thought Burnham had the North West at heart so obviously he will be sadly missed because of everything - the infrastructure that he tried to put into place, and Manchester has gone

similar_previous.semantic_text: Andy Burnham's win means change at the top for Greater Manchester Burnham victory in Makerfield means Greater Manchester must find a new regional mayor Burnham victory in Makerfield means Greater Manchester must find a new regional mayor Учитывать как новую фазу городской политики или public-affairs повестки сегодня. Sitting MPs cannot serve as regional mayors, because the regional role incorporates the role of police and crime commissioner The date for the Greater Manchester vote is 30 July Burnham, who won the Makerfield by-election for Labour with a 55% share of the vote, became the region's first directly elected mayor in 2017 , and won two further terms in 2021 and 2024 In 2024, he won 63.4% of the vote, with 420,749 voters backing him He won in every borough in the city region, including in Wigan, where the Makerfield constituency sits The cost of finding a new mayor to replace Burnham is estimated to be about £4.7m - which was one of the reasons Labour gave for blocking his bid to become its candidate in the Gorton and Denton by-election in February

The outgoing Greater Manchester mayor held off a challenge from Reform UK, behind by more than 9,000 votes
Burnham victory in Makerfield means Greater Manchester must find a new regional mayor
Residents react after the now ex-mayor of Greater Manchester stormed to victory in Makerfield

As Andy Burnham gave his acceptance speech on the stage after he swept to victory in the Makerfield by-election, he spoke of "some sadness" that the appointment brought to an end "my wonderful nine years as mayor of Greater Manchester" He said it was a "wrench" to have to leave t
evidence_texts: He said it was a "wrench" to have to leave the job he loved, although promised he was not "leaving the service of Greater Manchester" But what is his legacy according to people living in the region? Some residents in Salford told of their sadness and disappointment at his departure, after Burnham's return to Westminster politics automatically triggered a mayoral election Neil Townley, 59, from Worsley, said: "I'm quietly disappointed to be perfectly honest with you "I've always thought Burnham had the North West at heart so obviously he will be sadly missed because of everything - the infrastructure that he tried to put into place, and Manchester has gone from strength to strength since he's been in charge "He knows the people, he knows how we function, and basically, I wish him all the best, but he will be sadly missed."

Burnham said his win could mark a "turning point" for the country, while he pledged not to use the constituency as a stepping stone But Prime Minister Sir Keir defended his government's record on delivering change and insisted he would stand in any leadership contest, adding: "I'm not going to walk away." Burnham was joined by several Labour MPs at a victory rally on Friday, while his allies increased their calls for Sir Keir to stand aside and agree a "managed way forward" to avoid a bruising contest There were three by-election results overnight and in Aberdeen South, the Scottish Conservatives won a Westminster by-election for the first time in more than 50 years taking the seat from the SNP; while in Arbroath and Broughty Ferry the SNP held onto the seat in its by-election Burnham won the Makerfield constituency after securing nearly 55% of the vote The former Greater Manchester mayo

Sitting MPs cannot serve as regional mayors, because the regional role incorporates the role of police and crime commissioner The date for the Greater Manchester vote is 30 July Burnham, who won the Makerfield by-election for Labour with a 55% share of the vote, became the region's first directly elected mayor in 2017 , and won two further terms in 2021 and 2024 In 2024, he won 63.4% of the vote, with 420,749 voters backing him He won in every borough in the city region, including in Wigan, where the Makerfield constituency sits The cost of finding a new mayor to replace Burnham is estimated to be about £4.7m - which was one of the reasons Labour gave for blocking his bid to become its candidate in the Gorton and Denton by-election in February

Those were the words of Makerfield resident Carol Lamb, who was one of nearly 25,000 people who voted for Andy Burnham on Thursday Residents in the constituency have been reacting to the now ex-mayor of Greater Manchester's huge win in the by-election, saying he needs to deliver on what he pledged on the doorsteps in Wigan Burnham won a commanding victory as he secured 55% of the vote and a 9,000 majority over Reform UK's Robert Kenyon, with Reform failing to match May's strong local election results Lamb said Burnham now needed to "make sure that money is spent where it needs to be spent" Echoing her view, resident Marcin Wysocki, who owns a chicken shop in Ashton-in-Makerfield, said it was important for politicians to "deliver what they promised" While Reform were initially bullish about their chances in Makerfield, Burnham's popularity was also a source of optimism for Labour in an ar

Huge win in by-election paves way for Labour leadership challenge Economic Development Declan Carey Local Democracy Reporter 07:33, 19 Jun 2026 Andy Burnham Andy Burnham is heading back to Parliament after winning the Makerfield by-election The newly elected Labour MP came out on top in the race against a strong challenge from Reform UK candidate Robert Kenyon But overnight Mr Burnham secured a seismic win, with 24,927 votes placing him well ahead of Reform challenger Robert Kenyon who secured 15,696 votes. His return to Parliament is expected to pave the way for a leadership challenge against Prime Minister Keir Starmer It marks the end of weeks of campaigning in the constituency based south of Wigan, and the start of the next political race in Greater Manchester for Andy Burnham's old job as mayor Becoming an MP disqualifies Burnham from being Greater Manchester mayor, so a replacement needs to be found for the region's top political job

evidence_texts: Political parties have begun to announce their candidates for the forthcoming Greater Manchester mayoral by-election The by-election was triggered when Andy Burnham resigned as mayor following his return to the House of Commons after winning the Makerfield by-election on 18 June Labour's Burnham won the first mayoral election in 2017 and secured two further terms in office People living in all 10 boroughs of Greater Manchester will elect his successor on 30 July Here is a list (in alphabetical order, by surname) of all of the candidates who have confirmed they are standing in the election This list will be updated as and when further candidates are announced

Andy Burnham's victory in the Makerfield by-election means he has to step down as Greater Manchester mayor Burnham has been mayor since the role was first created in 2017 and the race to become his successor will be fiercely contested by all involved But what is the role they will be fighting for? The role of mayor was created as part of a law passed in 2016, one of a number of big pieces of devolution legislation brought in by the Conservatives after taking power in 2010 In a 2012 referendum, Manchester rejected having a directly-elected city mayor, along with eight other major cities, but the decision to have a regional or metropolitan mayor was directly negotiated between the government and the 10 Greater Manchester councils Andy Burnham, who left Parliament to stand for the role, was chosen as Labour's candidate for the first mayoral election in 2017 and won, taking 63.4% of the vote

Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade. Credit: PA Images Andy Burnham’s historic victory in the Makerfield by-election sets off a rapid chain reaction for both Greater Manchester and the wider UK political landscape A metro mayor cannot simultaneously serve as an MP, a clear legal and electoral process is now underway to find someone new to take on the role Andy Burnham has won a seat as an MP - but can he still be the Mayor of Greater Manchester? No. Andy Burnham cannot be mayor and a member of parliament. He had seven days - from the moment of his election - to resign from the post which he has held for almost a decade He handed in his resignation on 19 June - triggering the process of the by-election Burnham cannot do both jobs because the Mayor of Greater Manchester holds Police and Crime Commissioner (PCC) powers and UK l

Politics Andy Burnham sworn in as MP for Makerfield LIVE Updated just now Andy Burnham leadership bid LIVE as new Makerfield MP to set out stall to be the new Prime Minister after Keir Starmer resigns Sir Keir Starmer resigned on Monday with the prosp Sir Keir Starmer yesterday (
evidence_texts: Just hours earlier, Sir Keir Starmer announced his resignation both as prime minister and Labour leader Former Greater Manchester Mayor Burnham, who won the Makerfield by-election by 9,000 votes last week after seeing off a challenge from Reform UK, has confirmed he wants to succeed Starmer The by-election was triggered when Labour's Josh Simons stood down to make way for Burnham, who represented the neighbouring Greater Manchester constituency of Leigh between 2001 until 2017, when he became mayor Burnham bowed his head several times as he made his way through the Commons chamber and grinned as he was greeted by his colleagues Sir Desmond Swayne, a Conservative former minister, heckled as Burnham approached the clerks to be sworn in In another heckle, MPs were told that "he's not the messiah" and there was a shout of "water into wine"

Andy Burnham has been officially sworn in as Makerfield's new MP amid loud cheers from his Labour colleagues in the House of Commons Just hours earlier, Sir Keir Starmer announced his resignation both as prime minister and Labour leader Former Greater Manchester Mayor Burnham, who won the Makerfield by-election by 9,000 votes last week after seeing off a challenge from Reform UK, has confirmed he wants to succeed Starmer The by-election was triggered when Labour's Josh Simons stood down to make way for Burnham, who represented the neighbouring Greater Manchester constituency of Leigh between 2001 until 2017, when he became mayor Burnham bowed his head several times as he made his way through the Commons chamber and grinned as he was greeted by his colleagues Sir Desmond Swayne, a Conservative former minister, heckled as Burnham approached the clerks to be sworn in

Political parties have begun to announce their candidates for the forthcoming Greater Manchester mayoral by-election The by-election was triggered when Andy Burnham resigned as mayor following his return to the House of Commons after winning the Makerfield by-election on 18 June Labour's Burnham won the first mayoral election in 2017 and secured two further terms in office People living in all 10 boroughs of Greater Manchester will elect his successor on 30 July Here is a list (in alphabetical order, by surname) of all of the candidates who have confirmed they are standing in the election This list will be updated as and when further candidates are announced

As Andy Burnham gave his acceptance speech on the stage after he swept to victory in the Makerfield by-election, he spoke of "some sadness" that the appointment brought to an end "my wonderful nine years as mayor of Greater Manchester" He said it was a "wrench" to have to leave the job he loved, although promised he was not "leaving the service of Greater Manchester" But what is his legacy according to people living in the region? Some residents in Salford told of their sadness and disappointment at his departure, after Burnham's return to Westminster politics automatically triggered a mayoral election Neil Townley, 59, from Worsley, said: "I'm quietly disappointed to be perfectly honest with you "I've always thought Burnham had the North West at heart so obviously he will be sadly missed because of everything - the infrastructure that he tried to put into place, and Manchester has gone

similar_previous.semantic_text: Andy Burnham's win means change at the top for Greater Manchester Burnham victory in Makerfield means Greater Manchester must find a new regional mayor Burnham victory in Makerfield means Greater Manchester must find a new regional mayor Учитывать как новую фазу городской политики или public-affairs повестки сегодня. Sitting MPs cannot serve as regional mayors, because the regional role incorporates the role of police and crime commissioner The date for the Greater Manchester vote is 30 July Burnham, who won the Makerfield by-election for Labour with a 55% share of the vote, became the region's first directly elected mayor in 2017 , and won two further terms in 2021 and 2024 In 2024, he won 63.4% of the vote, with 420,749 voters backing him He won in every borough in the city region, including in Wigan, where the Makerfield constituency sits The cost of finding a new mayor to replace Burnham is estimated to be about £4.7m - which was one of the reasons Labour gave for blocking his bid to become its candidate in the Gorton and Denton by-election in February

evidence_texts: He said it was a "wrench" to have to leave the job he loved, although promised he was not "leaving the service of Greater Manchester" But what is his legacy according to people living in the region? Some residents in Salford told of their sadness and disappointment at his departure, after Burnham's return to Westminster politics automatically triggered a mayoral election Neil Townley, 59, from Worsley, said: "I'm quietly disappointed to be perfectly honest with you "I've always thought Burnham had the North West at heart so obviously he will be sadly missed because of everything - the infrastructure that he tried to put into place, and Manchester has gone from strength to strength since he's been in charge "He knows the people, he knows how we function, and basically, I wish him all the best, but he will be sadly missed."

Burnham said his win could mark a "turning point" for the country, while he pledged not to use the constituency as a stepping stone But Prime Minister Sir Keir defended his government's record on delivering change and insisted he would stand in any leadership contest, adding: "I'm not going to walk away." Burnham was joined by several Labour MPs at a victory rally on Friday, while his allies increased their calls for Sir Keir to stand aside and agree a "managed way forward" to avoid a bruising contest There were three by-election results overnight and in Aberdeen South, the Scottish Conservatives won a Westminster by-election for the first time in more than 50 years taking the seat from the SNP; while in Arbroath and Broughty Ferry the SNP held onto the seat in its by-election Burnham won the Makerfield constituency after securing nearly 55% of the vote The former Greater Manchester mayo

Sitting MPs cannot serve as regional mayors, because the regional role incorporates the role of police and crime commissioner The date for the Greater Manchester vote is 30 July Burnham, who won the Makerfield by-election for Labour with a 55% share of the vote, became the region's first directly elected mayor in 2017 , and won two further terms in 2021 and 2024 In 2024, he won 63.4% of the vote, with 420,749 voters backing him He won in every borough in the city region, including in Wigan, where the Makerfield constituency sits The cost of finding a new mayor to replace Burnham is estimated to be about £4.7m - which was one of the reasons Labour gave for blocking his bid to become its candidate in the Gorton and Denton by-election in February

Those were the words of Makerfield resident Carol Lamb, who was one of nearly 25,000 people who voted for Andy Burnham on Thursday Residents in the constituency have been reacting to the now ex-mayor of Greater Manchester's huge win in the by-election, saying he needs to deliver on what he pledged on the doorsteps in Wigan Burnham won a commanding victory as he secured 55% of the vote and a 9,000 majority over Reform UK's Robert Kenyon, with Reform failing to match May's strong local election results Lamb said Burnham now needed to "make sure that money is spent where it needs to be spent" Echoing her view, resident Marcin Wysocki, who owns a chicken shop in Ashton-in-Makerfield, said it was important for politicians to "deliver what they promised" While Reform were initially bullish about their chances in Makerfield, Burnham's popularity was also a source of optimism for Labour in an ar

evidence_packet.evidence_text: Economic Development Andy Burnham wins Makerfield: When will the next Greater Manchester mayor be elected now he is an MP? Huge win in by-election paves way for Labour leadership challenge Economic Development Declan Carey Local Democracy Reporter 07:33, 19 Jun 2026 Andy Burnham Andy Burnham is heading back to Parliament after winning the Makerfield by-election The newly elected Labour MP came out on top in the race against a strong challenge from Reform UK candidate Robert Kenyon But overnight Mr Burnham secured a seismic win, with 24,927 votes placing him well ahead of Reform challenger Robert Kenyon who secured 15,696 votes. His return to Parliament is expected to pave the way for a leadership challenge against Prime Minister Keir Starmer It marks the end of weeks of campaigning in the constituency based south of Wigan, and the start of the next political race in Greater Manchester for Andy Burnham's old job as mayor Becoming an MP disqualifies Burnham from being Greater Manchester mayor, so a replacement needs to be found for the region's top political job

evidence_texts: Political parties have begun to announce their candidates for the forthcoming Greater Manchester mayoral by-election The by-election was triggered when Andy Burnham resigned as mayor following his return to the House of Commons after winning the Makerfield by-election on 18 June Labour's Burnham won the first mayoral election in 2017 and secured two further terms in office People living in all 10 boroughs of Greater Manchester will elect his successor on 30 July Here is a list (in alphabetical order, by surname) of all of the candidates who have confirmed they are standing in the election This list will be updated as and when further candidates are announced

Andy Burnham's victory in the Makerfield by-election means he has to step down as Greater Manchester mayor Burnham has been mayor since the role was first created in 2017 and the race to become his successor will be fiercely contested by all involved But what is the role they will be fighting for? The role of mayor was created as part of a law passed in 2016, one of a number of big pieces of devolution legislation brought in by the Conservatives after taking power in 2010 In a 2012 referendum, Manchester rejected having a directly-elected city mayor, along with eight other major cities, but the decision to have a regional or metropolitan mayor was directly negotiated between the government and the 10 Greater Manchester councils Andy Burnham, who left Parliament to stand for the role, was chosen as Labour's candidate for the first mayoral election in 2017 and won, taking 63.4% of the vote

Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade. Credit: PA Images Andy Burnham’s historic victory in the Makerfield by-election sets off a rapid chain reaction for both Greater Manchester and the wider UK political landscape A metro mayor cannot simultaneously serve as an MP, a clear legal and electoral process is now underway to find someone new to take on the role Andy Burnham has won a seat as an MP - but can he still be the Mayor of Greater Manchester? No. Andy Burnham cannot be mayor and a member of parliament. He had seven days - from the moment of his election - to resign from the post which he has held for almost a decade He handed in his resignation on 19 June - triggering the process of the by-election Burnham cannot do both jobs because the Mayor of Greater Manchester holds Police and Crime Commissioner (PCC) powers and UK l

The role of mayor was created as part of a law passed in 2016, one of a number of big pieces of devolution legislation brought in by the Conservatives after taking power in 2010 In a 2012 referendum, Manchester rejected having a directly-elected city mayor, along with eight other major cities, but the decision to have a regional or metropolitan mayor was directly negotiated between the government and the 10 Greater Manchester councils Andy Burnham, who left Parliament to stand for the role, was chosen as Labour's candidate for the first mayoral election in 2017 and won, taking 63.4% of the vote

Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade. Credit: PA Images Andy Burnham’s historic victory in the Makerfield by-election sets off a rapid chain reaction for both Greater Manchester and the wider UK political landscape A metro mayor cannot simultaneously serve as an MP, a clear legal and electoral process is now underway to find someone new to take on the role Andy Burnham has won a seat as an MP - but can he still be the Mayor of Greater Manchester? No. Andy Burnham cannot be mayor and a member of parliament. He had seven days - from the moment of his election - to resign from the post which he has held for almost a decade He handed in his resignation on 19 June - triggering the process of the by-election Burnham cannot do both jobs because the Mayor of Greater Manchester holds Police and Crime Commissioner (PCC) powers and UK l

Economic Development Andy Burnham wins Makerfield: When will the next Greater Manchester mayor be elected now he is an MP? Huge win in by-election paves way for Labour leadership challenge Economic Development Declan Carey Local Democracy Reporter 07:33, 19 Jun 2026 Andy Burnham Andy Burnham is heading back to Parliament after winning the Makerfield by-election The newly elected Labour MP came out on top in the race against a strong challenge from Reform UK candidate Robert Kenyon But overnight Mr Burnham secured a seismic win, with 24,927 votes placing him well ahead of Reform challenger Robert Kenyon who secured 15,696 votes. His return to Parliament is expected to pave the way for a leadership challenge against Prime Minister Keir Starmer It marks the end of weeks of campaigning in the constituency based south of Wigan, and the start of the next political race in Greater Manchester fo

Just hours earlier, Sir Keir Starmer announced his resignation both as prime minister and Labour leader Former Greater Manchester Mayor Burnham, who won the Makerfield by-election by 9,000 votes last week after seeing off a challenge from Reform UK, has confirmed he wants to succeed Starmer The by-election was triggered when Labour's Josh Simons stood down to make way for Burnham, who represented the neighbouring Greater Manchester constituency of Leigh between 2001 until 2017, when he became mayor Burnham bowed his head several times as he made his way through the Commons chamber and grinned as he was greeted by his colleagues Sir Desmond Swayne, a Conservative former minister, heckled as Burnham approached the clerks to be sworn in In another heckle, MPs were told that "he's not the messiah" and there was a shout of "water into wine"

Andy Burnham has been officially sworn in as Makerfield's new MP amid loud cheers from his Labour colleagues in the House of Commons Just hours earlier, Sir Keir Starmer announced his resignation both as prime minister and Labour leader Former Greater Manchester Mayor Burnham, who won the Makerfield by-election by 9,000 votes last week after seeing off a challenge from Reform UK, has confirmed he wants to succeed Starmer The by-election was triggered when Labour's Josh Simons stood down to make way for Burnham, who represented the neighbouring Greater Manchester constituency of Leigh between 2001 until 2017, when he became mayor Burnham bowed his head several times as he made his way through the Commons chamber and grinned as he was greeted by his colleagues Sir Desmond Swayne, a Conservative former minister, heckled as Burnham approached the clerks to be sworn in

Political parties have begun to announce their candidates for the forthcoming Greater Manchester mayoral by-election The by-election was triggered when Andy Burnham resigned as mayor following his return to the House of Commons after winning the Makerfield by-election on 18 June Labour's Burnham won the first mayoral election in 2017 and secured two further terms in office People living in all 10 boroughs of Greater Manchester will elect his successor on 30 July Here is a list (in alphabetical order, by surname) of all of the candidates who have confirmed they are standing in the election This list will be updated as and when further candidates are announced

As Andy Burnham gave his acceptance speech on the stage after he swept to victory in the Makerfield by-election, he spoke of "some sadness" that the appointment brought to an end "my wonderful nine years as mayor of Greater Manchester" He said it was a "wrench" to have to leave the job he loved, although promised he was not "leaving the service of Greater Manchester" But what is his legacy according to people living in the region? Some residents in Salford told of their sadness and disappointment at his departure, after Burnham's return to Westminster politics automatically triggered a mayoral election Neil Townley, 59, from Worsley, said: "I'm quietly disappointed to be perfectly honest with you "I've always thought Burnham had the North West at heart so obviously he will be sadly missed because of everything - the infrastructure that he tried to put into place, and Manchester has gone

similar_previous.semantic_text: Andy Burnham's win means change at the top for Greater Manchester Burnham victory in Makerfield means Greater Manchester must find a new regional mayor Burnham victory in Makerfield means Greater Manchester must find a new regional mayor Учитывать как новую фазу городской политики или public-affairs повестки сегодня. Sitting MPs cannot serve as regional mayors, because the regional role incorporates the role of police and crime commissioner The date for the Greater Manchester vote is 30 July Burnham, who won the Makerfield by-election for Labour with a 55% share of the vote, became the region's first directly elected mayor in 2017 , and won two further terms in 2021 and 2024 In 2024, he won 63.4% of the vote, with 420,749 voters backing him He won in every borough in the city region, including in Wigan, where the Makerfield constituency sits The cost of finding a new mayor to replace Burnham is estimated to be about £4.7m - which was one of the reasons Labour gave for blocking his bid to become its candidate in the Gorton and Denton by-election in February

summaries: As Andy Burnham becomes MP for Makerfield, Greater Manchester reacts to his departure as mayor
The outgoing Greater Manchester mayor held off a challenge from Reform UK, behind by more than 9,000 votes
Burnham victory in Makerfield means Greater Manchester must find a new regional mayor
Residents react after the now ex-mayor of Greater Manchester stormed to victory in Makerfield

As Andy Burnham gave his acceptance speech on the stage after he swept to victory in the Makerfield by-election, he spoke of "some sadness" that the appointment brought to an end "my wonderful nine years as mayor of Greater Manchester" He said it was a "wrench" to have to leave t
evidence_texts: He said it was a "wrench" to have to leave the job he loved, although promised he was not "leaving the service of Greater Manchester" But what is his legacy according to people living in the region? Some residents in Salford told of their sadness and disappointment at his departure, after Burnham's return to Westminster politics automatically triggered a mayoral election Neil Townley, 59, from Worsley, said: "I'm quietly disappointed to be perfectly honest with you "I've always thought Burnham had the North West at heart so obviously he will be sadly missed because of everything - the infrastructure that he tried to put into place, and Manchester has gone from strength to strength since he's been in charge "He knows the people, he knows how we function, and basically, I wish him all the best, but he will be sadly missed."

Burnham said his win could mark a "turning point" for the country, while he pledged not to use the constituency as a stepping stone But Prime Minister Sir Keir defended his government's record on delivering change and insisted he would stand in any leadership contest, adding: "I'm not going to walk away." Burnham was joined by several Labour MPs at a victory rally on Friday, while his allies increased their calls for Sir Keir to stand aside and agree a "managed way forward" to avoid a bruising contest There were three by-election results overnight and in Aberdeen South, the Scottish Conservatives won a Westminster by-election for the first time in more than 50 years taking the seat from the SNP; while in Arbroath and Broughty Ferry the SNP held onto the seat in its by-election Burnham won the Makerfield constituency after securing nearly 55% of the vote The former Greater Manchester mayo

Sitting MPs cannot serve as regional mayors, because the regional role incorporates the role of police and crime commissioner The date for the Greater Manchester vote is 30 July Burnham, who won the Makerfield by-election for Labour with a 55% share of the vote, became the region's first directly elected mayor in 2017 , and won two further terms in 2021 and 2024 In 2024, he won 63.4% of the vote, with 420,749 voters backing him He won in every borough in the city region, including in Wigan, where the Makerfield constituency sits The cost of finding a new mayor to replace Burnham is estimated to be about £4.7m - which was one of the reasons Labour gave for blocking his bid to become its candidate in the Gorton and Denton by-election in February

Those were the words of Makerfield resident Carol Lamb, who was one of nearly 25,000 people who voted for Andy Burnham on Thursday Residents in the constituency have been reacting to the now ex-mayor of Greater Manchester's huge win in the by-election, saying he needs to deliver on what he pledged on the doorsteps in Wigan Burnham won a commanding victory as he secured 55% of the vote and a 9,000 majority over Reform UK's Robert Kenyon, with Reform failing to match May's strong local election results Lamb said Burnham now needed to "make sure that money is spent where it needs to be spent" Echoing her view, resident Marcin Wysocki, who owns a chicken shop in Ashton-in-Makerfield, said it was important for politicians to "deliver what they promised" While Reform were initially bullish about their chances in Makerfield, Burnham's popularity was also a source of optimism for Labour in an ar

Makerfield by-election 2026 LIVE Updated 4 mins ago Makerfield by-election LIVE as Andy Burnham and Rob Kenyon joined by major Labour and Reform figures as polls open The latest updates and news as polling stations open across the constituency of Make The highly anticipated Makerfield by-election comes after the sudden resignation of Labour MP Josh Simons on May 14, with the Makerfield MP having confirmed he stood down from the position to make a pathway for Greater Manchester mayor Andy Burnham to stand for election Simons' resignation followed mounting demands for Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer to resign, with Mr Burnham expected to launch a leadership challenge against Sir Keir if elected . This means the outcome of the by-election could shape the UK political scene for years to come There are 14 candidates running to be the new Member of Parliament in the Wigan constituency : Jake A

The result is expected early on Friday There are 14 candidates standing in the by-election, which was triggered by the resignation of Labour MP Josh Simons It is one of three Westminster by-elections taking place, with voters in the Scottish constituencies of Arbroath and Broughty Ferry and Aberdeen South also heading to the polls after their MPs stood down to take up posts as MSPs following May's Holyrood elections Voters have been reminded to bring a valid form of identification with them A passport, driving licence, bus pass, blue badge and a voter authority certificate are all accepted Photo IDs that are out of date can still be used if the image still looks like you

People living in the Greater Manchester constituency of Makerfield are heading to the polls to elect their new MP More than 30 polling stations across the parliamentary constituency opened at 07:00 BST and will close at 22:00, after which the count will take place. The result is expected early on Friday There are 14 candidates standing in the by-election, which was triggered by the resignation of Labour MP Josh Simons It is one of three Westminster by-elections taking place, with voters in the Scottish constituencies of Arbroath and Broughty Ferry and Aberdeen South also heading to the polls after their MPs stood down to take up posts as MSPs following May's Holyrood elections Voters have been reminded to bring a valid form of identification with them A passport, driving licence, bus pass, blue badge and a voter authority certificate are all accepted

Makerfield by-election 2026 When the next General Election is set to take place following Makerfield by-election All of the eyes of Westminster will be on the outcome of the Makerfield contest News Ryan Price 12:23, 18 Jun 2026 View 2 Images Sir Keir As the polls open for the much-talked about Makerfield by-election today (Thursday, 18 June), questions are mounting across the country regarding the exact timeline for the next general election The extraordinary circumstances surrounding the Makerfield vote - which was triggered by the resignation of Labour MP Josh Simons last month to clear a path for Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham to re-enter Parliament - have fundamentally shaken the political landscape With Westminster bracing for potential leadership challenges and a surging Reform UK opposition, speculation regarding the next date the public will head to the polls is dominating

Makerfield by-election 2026 LIVE Updated 4 mins ago Makerfield by-election LIVE as Andy Burnham and Rob Kenyon joined by major Labour and Reform figures as polls open The latest updates and news as polling stations open across the constituency of Make The highly anticipated Makerfield by-election comes after the sudden resignation of Labour MP Josh Simons on May 14, with the Makerfield MP having confirmed he stood down from the position to make a pathway for Greater Manchester mayor Andy Burnham to stand for election Simons' resignation followed mounting demands for Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer to resign, with Mr Burnham expected to launch a leadership challenge against Sir Keir if elected . This means the outcome of the by-election could shape the UK political scene for years to come There are 14 candidates running to be the new Member of Parliament in the Wigan constituency : Jake A

evidence_packet.lead: Manchester terror attack 'Saffie would be amazed - she'd absolutely love it' Saffie Roussos' parents are helping other children pursue their love of performing Comments News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 12:03, 03 Jun 2026 View Image Saffie Roussos (Image: The parents of the youngest
evidence_packet.summary: Manchester terror attack 'Saffie would be amazed - she'd absolutely love it' Saffie Roussos' parents are helping other children pursue their love of performing Comments News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 12:03, 03 Jun 2026 View Image Saffie Roussos (Image: The parents of the youngest
evidence_packet.evidence_text: Manchester terror attack 'Saffie would be amazed - she'd absolutely love it' Saffie Roussos' parents are helping other children pursue their love of performing Comments News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 12:03, 03 Jun 2026 View Image Saffie Roussos (Image: The parents of the youngest victim of the Manchester Arena terror attack have told how they have found comfort by helping other children pursue their love of performing. Saffie Roussos was just eight-years-old when she was one of the 22 people killed during the 2017 attack follow Now her parents Andrew and Lisa are once again honouring their daughter’s legacy by celebrating an extraordinary young person who has gone above and beyond for others. For the third year running they've teamed up with The Sun and the Pauline Quirke Academy of Performing Arts to l The winner will also receive a a £5,000 holiday voucher from On The Beach. Mr Roussos told The Sun: “She’d absolutely love it. Saffie was always such a performer and she loved entertaining people. She was so generous and such a giver “The idea of helping another child, especially one performing on stage someday, she’d be amazed by that. It just feels right and it’s so fitting for who she was.” Charlie Quirke, son of Birds of a Feather star Pauline Quirke, added: “It is incredibly moving to see Saffie’s memory celebrated in such a joyful and generous way. Now, in what would have been her 18th year, the Saffie’s Smile Awards feel more meaningful than ever."

evidence_packet.evidence_text: Manchester terror attack 'Saffie would be amazed - she'd absolutely love it' Saffie Roussos' parents are helping other children pursue their love of performing Comments News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 12:03, 03 Jun 2026 View Image Saffie Roussos (Image: The parents of the youngest victim of the Manchester Arena terror attack have told how they have found comfort by helping other children pursue their love of performing. Saffie Roussos was just eight-years-old when she was one of the 22 people killed during the 2017 attack follow Now her parents Andrew and Lisa are once again honouring their daughter’s legacy by celebrating an extraordinary young person who has gone above and beyond for others. For the third year running they've teamed up with The Sun and the Pauline Quirke Academy of Performing Arts to l The winner will also receive a a £5,000 holiday voucher from On The Beach. Mr Roussos told The Sun: “She’d absolutely love it. Saffie was always such a performer and she loved entertaining people. She was so generous and such a giver “The idea of helping another child, especially one performing on stage someday, she’d be amazed by that. It just feels right and it’s so fitting for who she was.” Charlie Quirke, son of Birds of a Feather star Pauline Quirke, added: “It is incredibly moving to see Saffie’s memory celebrated in such a joyful and generous way. Now, in what would have been her 18th year, the Saffie’s Smile Awards feel more meaningful than ever."

Cycle racks are available on-site

Andy Burnham sworn in as new Makerfield MP
similar_previous.normalized_title: andy burnham sworn in as new makerfield mp
similar_previous.semantic_text: Andy Burnham sworn in as new Makerfield MP The former Greater Manchester mayor is seen as favourite to succeed Sir Keir Starmer as prime minister The former Greater Manchester mayor is seen as favourite to succeed Sir Keir Starmer as prime minister Учитывать, что сегодня это влияет на повестку городской политики. Just hours earlier, Sir Keir Starmer announced his resignation both as prime minister and Labour leader Former Greater Manchester Mayor Burnham, who won the Makerfield by-election by 9,000 votes last week after seeing off a challenge from Reform UK, has confirmed he wants to succeed Starmer The by-election was triggered when Labour's Josh Simons stood down to make way for Burnham, who represented the neighbouring Greater Manchester constituency of Leigh between 2001 until 2017, when he became mayor Burnham bowed his head several times as he made his way through the Commons chamber and grinned as he was greeted by his colleagues Sir Desmond Swayne, a Conservative former minister, heckled as Burnham approached the clerks to be sworn in In another heckle, MPs were told that "he's not the messiah" and there was a shout of "water into wine"

evidence_texts: Just hours earlier, Sir Keir Starmer announced his resignation both as prime minister and Labour leader Former Greater Manchester Mayor Burnham, who won the Makerfield by-election by 9,000 votes last week after seeing off a challenge from Reform UK, has confirmed he wants to succeed Starmer The by-election was triggered when Labour's Josh Simons stood down to make way for Burnham, who represented the neighbouring Greater Manchester constituency of Leigh between 2001 until 2017, when he became mayor Burnham bowed his head several times as he made his way through the Commons chamber and grinned as he was greeted by his colleagues Sir Desmond Swayne, a Conservative former minister, heckled as Burnham approached the clerks to be sworn in In another heckle, MPs were told that "he's not the messiah" and there was a shout of "water into wine"

Andy Burnham has been officially sworn in as Makerfield's new MP amid loud cheers from his Labour colleagues in the House of Commons Just hours earlier, Sir Keir Starmer announced his resignation both as prime minister and Labour leader Former Greater Manchester Mayor Burnham, who won the Makerfield by-election by 9,000 votes last week after seeing off a challenge from Reform UK, has confirmed he wants to succeed Starmer The by-election was triggered when Labour's Josh Simons stood down to make way for Burnham, who represented the neighbouring Greater Manchester constituency of Leigh between 2001 until 2017, when he became mayor Burnham bowed his head several times as he made his way through the Commons chamber and grinned as he was greeted by his colleagues Sir Desmond Swayne, a Conservative former minister, heckled as Burnham approached the clerks to be sworn in

Political parties have begun to announce their candidates for the forthcoming Greater Manchester mayoral by-election The by-election was triggered when Andy Burnham resigned as mayor following his return to the House of Commons after winning the Makerfield by-election on 18 June Labour's Burnham won the first mayoral election in 2017 and secured two further terms in office People living in all 10 boroughs of Greater Manchester will elect his successor on 30 July Here is a list (in alphabetical order, by surname) of all of the candidates who have confirmed they are standing in the election This list will be updated as and when further candidates are announced

As Andy Burnham gave his acceptance speech on the stage after he swept to victory in the Makerfield by-election, he spoke of "some sadness" that the appointment brought to an end "my wonderful nine years as mayor of Greater Manchester" He said it was a "wrench" to have to leave the job he loved, although promised he was not "leaving the service of Greater Manchester" But what is his legacy according to people living in the region? Some residents in Salford told of their sadness and disappointment at his departure, after Burnham's return to Westminster politics automatically triggered a mayoral election Neil Townley, 59, from Worsley, said: "I'm quietly disappointed to be perfectly honest with you "I've always thought Burnham had the North West at heart so obviously he will be sadly missed because of everything - the infrastructure that he tried to put into place, and Manchester has gone

similar_previous.semantic_text: Andy Burnham's win means change at the top for Greater Manchester Burnham victory in Makerfield means Greater Manchester must find a new regional mayor Burnham victory in Makerfield means Greater Manchester must find a new regional mayor Учитывать как новую фазу городской политики или public-affairs повестки сегодня. Sitting MPs cannot serve as regional mayors, because the regional role incorporates the role of police and crime commissioner The date for the Greater Manchester vote is 30 July Burnham, who won the Makerfield by-election for Labour with a 55% share of the vote, became the region's first directly elected mayor in 2017 , and won two further terms in 2021 and 2024 In 2024, he won 63.4% of the vote, with 420,749 voters backing him He won in every borough in the city region, including in Wigan, where the Makerfield constituency sits The cost of finding a new mayor to replace Burnham is estimated to be about £4.7m - which was one of the reasons Labour gave for blocking his bid to become its candidate in the Gorton and Denton by-election in February

Some residents in Salford told of their sadness and disappointment at his departure, after Burnham's return to Westminster politics automatically triggered a mayoral election Neil Townley, 59, from Worsley, said: "I'm quietly disappointed to be perfectly honest with you "I've always thought Burnham had the North West at heart so obviously he will be sadly missed because of everything - the infrastructure that he tried to put into place, and Manchester has gone from strength to strength since he's been in charge "He knows the people, he knows how we function, and basically, I wish him all the best, but he will be sadly missed."

Burnham said his win could mark a "turning point" for the country, while he pledged not to use the constituency as a stepping stone But Prime Minister Sir Keir defended his government's record on delivering change and insisted he would stand in any leadership contest, adding: "I'm not going to walk away." Burnham was joined by several Labour MPs at a victory rally on Friday, while his allies increased their calls for Sir Keir to stand aside and agree a "managed way forward" to avoid a bruising contest There were three by-election results overnight and in Aberdeen South, the Scottish Conservatives won a Westminster by-election for the first time in more than 50 years taking the seat from the SNP; while in Arbroath and Broughty Ferry the SNP held onto the seat in its by-election Burnham won the Makerfield constituency after securing nearly 55% of the vote The former Greater Manchester mayo

Sitting MPs cannot serve as regional mayors, because the regional role incorporates the role of police and crime commissioner The date for the Greater Manchester vote is 30 July Burnham, who won the Makerfield by-election for Labour with a 55% share of the vote, became the region's first directly elected mayor in 2017 , and won two further terms in 2021 and 2024 In 2024, he won 63.4% of the vote, with 420,749 voters backing him He won in every borough in the city region, including in Wigan, where the Makerfield constituency sits The cost of finding a new mayor to replace Burnham is estimated to be about £4.7m - which was one of the reasons Labour gave for blocking his bid to become its candidate in the Gorton and Denton by-election in February

Those were the words of Makerfield resident Carol Lamb, who was one of nearly 25,000 people who voted for Andy Burnham on Thursday Residents in the constituency have been reacting to the now ex-mayor of Greater Manchester's huge win in the by-election, saying he needs to deliver on what he pledged on the doorsteps in Wigan Burnham won a commanding victory as he secured 55% of the vote and a 9,000 majority over Reform UK's Robert Kenyon, with Reform failing to match May's strong local election results Lamb said Burnham now needed to "make sure that money is spent where it needs to be spent" Echoing her view, resident Marcin Wysocki, who owns a chicken shop in Ashton-in-Makerfield, said it was important for politicians to "deliver what they promised" While Reform were initially bullish about their chances in Makerfield, Burnham's popularity was also a source of optimism for Labour in an ar

evidence_texts: He said it was a "wrench" to have to leave the job he loved, although promised he was not "leaving the service of Greater Manchester" But what is his legacy according to people living in the region? Some residents in Salford told of their sadness and disappointment at his departure, after Burnham's return to Westminster politics automatically triggered a mayoral election Neil Townley, 59, from Worsley, said: "I'm quietly disappointed to be perfectly honest with you "I've always thought Burnham had the North West at heart so obviously he will be sadly missed because of everything - the infrastructure that he tried to put into place, and Manchester has gone from strength to strength since he's been in charge "He knows the people, he knows how we function, and basically, I wish him all the best, but he will be sadly missed."

The result is expected early on Friday There are 14 candidates standing in the by-election, which was triggered by the resignation of Labour MP Josh Simons It is one of three Westminster by-elections taking place, with voters in the Scottish constituencies of Arbroath and Broughty Ferry and Aberdeen South also heading to the polls after their MPs stood down to take up posts as MSPs following May's Holyrood elections Voters have been reminded to bring a valid form of identification with them A passport, driving licence, bus pass, blue badge and a voter authority certificate are all accepted Photo IDs that are out of date can still be used if the image still looks like you

The result is expected early on Friday There are 14 candidates standing in the by-election, which was triggered by the resignation of Labour MP Josh Simons It is one of three Westminster by-elections taking place, with voters in the Scottish constituencies of Arbroath and Broughty Ferry and Aberdeen South also heading to the polls after their MPs stood down to take up posts as MSPs following May's Holyrood elections Voters have been reminded to bring a valid form of identification with them A passport, driving licence, bus pass, blue badge and a voter authority certificate are all accepted

Makerfield by-election 2026 When the next General Election is set to take place following Makerfield by-election All of the eyes of Westminster will be on the outcome of the Makerfield contest News Ryan Price 12:23, 18 Jun 2026 View 2 Images Sir Keir As the polls open for the much-talked about Makerfield by-election today (Thursday, 18 June), questions are mounting across the country regarding the exact timeline for the next general election The extraordinary circumstances surrounding the Makerfield vote - which was triggered by the resignation of Labour MP Josh Simons last month to clear a path for Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham to re-enter Parliament - have fundamentally shaken the political landscape With Westminster bracing for potential leadership challenges and a surging Reform UK opposition, speculation regarding the next date the public will head to the polls is dominating

Makerfield by-election 2026 LIVE Updated 4 mins ago Makerfield by-election LIVE as Andy Burnham and Rob Kenyon joined by major Labour and Reform figures as polls open The latest updates and news as polling stations open across the constituency of Make The highly anticipated Makerfield by-election comes after the sudden resignation of Labour MP Josh Simons on May 14, with the Makerfield MP having confirmed he stood down from the position to make a pathway for Greater Manchester mayor Andy Burnham to stand for election Simons' resignation followed mounting demands for Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer to resign, with Mr Burnham expected to launch a leadership challenge against Sir Keir if elected . This means the outcome of the by-election could shape the UK political scene for years to come There are 14 candidates running to be the new Member of Parliament in the Wigan constituency : Jake A
